title: /brag — Launch Video
description: You built it. Now brag. Point it at a project URL (or upload the files) and it reads the project, plans a launch concept specific to it, storyboards it to the brag creative laws, composes it in Hyperframes, and renders a short, shareable brag.mp4 — with a postable caption. Local render, no API key.

cta.label: Fork and run
cta.note: Runs on Jetty's managed sandbox. No API key — Hyperframes renders locally (headless Chromium + FFmpeg). Free for your first 10 runs.

example_outputs.title: Horse Tinder
example_outputs.summary: A reference brag the source /brag project made for its Horse Tinder demo site — default tone: swipe through eligible horses near your pasture. Shown to illustrate the output style this runbook produces.

input.concept: Horse Tinder — a dating app for horses

outputs.description: The angle, hook, and beat-by-beat storyboard.

example_outputs.title: Taxi for Taxis
example_outputs.summary: A reference brag for the source project's Taxi for Taxis site — yc-parody tone, the absurd premise played completely straight: the ride-hailing app for ride-hailing assets, available in 12 metros.

input.concept: Taxi for Taxis — ride-hailing for ride-hailing assets

outputs.description: The angle, hook, and beat-by-beat storyboard.

example_outputs.title: Fish Flight School
example_outputs.summary: A reference brag for the source project's Fish Flight School site — cinematic/deadpan tone, trailer-scale restraint: the sky was never the limit. Illustrates the runbook's range across tones.

input.concept: Fish Flight School — teaching fish to fly

outputs.description: The angle, hook, and beat-by-beat storyboard.

inputs.label: Project URL
inputs.description: The live project to brag about. Captured with `hyperframes capture`. Provide this OR upload the project files.

inputs.label: Project files
inputs.description: Alternatively, upload index.html / styles.css / README — used directly when no URL is given.

inputs.label: Tone
inputs.description: A preset (default / polished / yc-parody / chaotic / deadpan / cinematic / app-store) or a freeform direction like 'fake Series A launch from 2016'.

secrets.description: Optional. Enables Gemini frame analysis on `hyperframes snapshot` for a stronger visual self-check. The render needs no API key.

attribution.author: latent-spaces — github.com/latent-spaces/brag
attribution.note: /brag is a Claude Code skill. This runbook adapts it to run in the Jetty sandbox: it captures a project URL (or reads uploaded files) instead of the local working directory, clones the source repo at run time for its bundled music + SFX + step references, and renders locally with `npx hyperframes`. Music by ende.app, SFX by Kenney, video generation by Hyperframes, demo sites by Impeccable — per the source repo credits.

caveats: The three example thumbnails are reference brags the source /brag project rendered for its own benchmark demo sites (Horse Tinder, Taxi for Taxis, Fish Flight School) — they illustrate the output style. The runbook itself is validated end-to-end on Jetty: a production run rendered a 20s brag.mp4 for jetty.io (claude-code via OpenRouter, draft quality, single ~11-min pass, lean artifacts).

Video rendering is heavier than a text runbook: it installs FFmpeg at run time, drives headless Chromium, and renders frame-by-frame — budget several minutes, and fall back to `--quality draft` if a high-quality render is too slow.
Bundled music/SFX are not shipped into this repo; the runbook clones github.com/latent-spaces/brag at run time to use them. If the clone fails (no network), the video renders silent.
Best on projects with a real visual identity and, ideally, a working flow to show. A bare landing page still works but leans on its strongest single visual.

handoff.name: brag
handoff.note: Converted from latent-spaces/brag. Validated end-to-end on Jetty (jetty-corp/brag-brave-reef) — rendered a 20s brag.mp4 for jetty.io. Example thumbnails are reference brags from the source project.
